label: Spring
description:
Adds springy secondary motion (overshoot and oscillation) to an animated piece, like Houdini's Spring motion effect. A virtual mass on a spring chases the input's animation: while the input moves the spring lags behind, and when the input stops the mass overshoots and oscillates before settling. Wire an animated shape into Geometry In and enable Animation, then scrub/play the timeline.

Spring Mode picks how it springs: LINEAR springs the piece's position (great for a shape that flies in and settles). TORSION springs its ANGLE about a pivot - a real torsion spring at the rotation centre, so a rotating arm or pendulum overshoots its swing and wobbles rotationally, physically accurate like a mechanical hinge spring. AUTO TORSION is the same but the pivot is detected automatically by fitting the arc the piece travels along (use it when the piece rotates about an external point). For TORSION, set Pivot X / Pivot Y to the rotation centre (the point you rotate around).

Spring Constant sets strength - higher gives faster, tighter oscillations. Mass is the weight on the spring - heavier oscillates slower, swings wider, and resists damping. Damping is resistance - higher kills the wobble sooner. TIP: the default settings give only a subtle wobble; for an obvious pendulum swing try lower stiffness + higher mass + low damping (e.g. Spring Constant 25, Mass 3, Damping 2), or push Amount above 1 to exaggerate the overshoot. The sim is baked deterministically from frame 0, so scrubbing and export are stable. This node only moves/rotates the piece; it never changes its shape.
